title: Assertion `pSub->pGroupBy==0' failed.
description:
yongheng added on 2020-01-06 15:34:36:
Consider the following test case:
CREATE TABLE v0 ( v1 INTEGER PRIMARY KEY ) ;
CREATE VIEW v2 ( v4 , v3 ) AS SELECT DISTINCT v1 , v1 FROM v0 WHERE ( v1 = 10 ) OR ( v1 = 10 ) OR ( v1 BETWEEN 0 AND 0 ) OR ( v1 = 10 ) OR ( v1 BETWEEN 10 AND 9223372036854775808 ) OR ( v1 = 10 ) OR ( v1 = 10 ) OR ( v1 = 10 ) OR ( v1 = 10 ) OR 10 >= v1 ORDER BY v1 ASC , v1 ASC ;
SELECT * FROM v0 AS a JOIN v2 ON ( SELECT 'LG CASEaaaaaaaaaaaa' FROM v2 AS y WHERE v2 . v4 = v4 GROUP BY v4 ) = v0 . v1 JOIN v0 AS b ON julianday () NATURAL JOIN v0 NATURAL JOIN v2 ;
When compiling with -DSQLITE_DEBUG, it triggers a fail in release and develop version of sqlite
yongheng added on 2020-01-06 17:31:47:
Why is this ticket marked as closed even it got a check-in to fix it?
drh added on 2020-01-06 17:42:55:
The "Resolution" is "Fixed".  "Closed" and "Fixed" mean essentially the same
thing.
yongheng added on 2020-01-06 17:47:59:
I see. Thanks.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE v0 ( v1 INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, v2 VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, v3 FLOAT DEFAULT 0.0 );
INSERT INTO v0 (v1, v2, v3) VALUES (10, 'test', 1.5), (20, NULL, 2.5), (30, 'example', NULL);
CREATE VIEW v2 AS SELECT v1, v2 FROM v0 WHERE v1 < 25 OR v2 IS NULL;
SELECT * FROM v0 AS a JOIN v2 ON a.v1 = (SELECT MAX(v1) FROM v0 WHERE v2 IS NOT NULL) JOIN v0 AS b ON b.v1 = a.v1;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM v2 WHERE v1 BETWEEN 0 AND 30 GROUP BY v2;
key_bug_features: JOIN with GROUP BY on a view causes assertion failure
